docs.title: Gatus Monitor
docs.text: Welcome to Gatus Monitor documentation!

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Gatus Monitor is a cross-platform system tray application that monitors multiple Gatus health check endpoints and provides visual feedback on their status through a color-coded icon.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Multi-endpoint monitoring: Monitor multiple Gatus instances simultaneously
  • Visual status indicators: Color-coded system tray icon (green/orange/red)
  • Configurable intervals: Set custom query intervals (default: 60s)
  • Staggered queries: Intelligent query distribution to avoid server load spikes
  • Cross-platform: Works on Linux, Windows, and macOS
  • Lightweight: Minimal resource usage (<50MB RAM, <1% CPU when idle)
  • Easy configuration: Simple settings panel for managing endpoints

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
1. Download the latest release for your platform
  2. Launch the application - it will appear in your system tray
  3. Right-click the tray icon and select "Settings"
  4. Add your Gatus URLs and configure the query interval
  5. Save and enjoy automatic monitoring!

docs.title: Status Indicators
docs.text: Icon Color Meaning Condition Green All systems operational 0 errors across all endpoints Orange Minor issues detected 1-2 errors on any endpoint Red Critical issues detected 3+ errors on any endpoint

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide will help you get started with Gatus Monitor.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: See the Installation Guide for detailed installation instructions for your platform.

docs.title: First Run
docs.text:
1. Launch the application

After installation, launch Gatus Monitor. The application will start minimized to your system tray.

  1. Locate the tray icon

Look for the Gatus Monitor icon in your system tray: - Windows: Bottom-right corner (notification area) - macOS: Top-right corner (menu bar) - Linux: Varies by desktop environment (typically top or bottom panel)

  1. Initial status

The icon will be green initially, indicating no endpoints are configured.

docs.title: Configuring Endpoints
docs.text:
1. Open Settings

Right-click the tray icon and select "Settings" from the menu.

  1. Add Gatus URLs

In the settings window: - Click "Add URL" - Enter your Gatus instance URL (e.g., https://status.example.com) - Click "Add" - Repeat for additional endpoints

  1. Configure Query Interval

Set how often Gatus Monitor should check your endpoints: - Default: 60 seconds - Minimum: 10 seconds - Maximum: 3600 seconds (1 hour)

  1. Save Configuration

Click "Save" to apply your changes. Monitoring will start automatically.

docs.title: Understanding the Status
docs.text:
The tray icon color indicates the overall health status:

  • Green: All endpoints are healthy (no errors)
  • Orange: Some endpoints have 1-2 errors
  • Red: One or more endpoints have 3+ errors or are unreachable

docs.title: Tooltip Information
docs.text:
Hover over the tray icon to see detailed status information:

  • Overall status
  • Number of endpoints
  • Healthy, unreachable, and error counts

docs.title: Manual Refresh
docs.text:
To force an immediate refresh of all endpoints:

  1. Right-click the tray icon
  2. Select "Refresh Now"

This will query all endpoints immediately, regardless of the configured interval.

docs.title: Exiting the Application
docs.text:
To exit Gatus Monitor:

  1. Right-click the tray icon
  2. Select "Quit"

Your configuration will be saved automatically.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Learn about Configuration Options
  • Troubleshoot issues in the Troubleshooting Guide
  • Check the FAQ for common questions

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Gatus Monitor is available for Linux, Windows, and macOS.

docs.title: Ubuntu/Debian (.deb)
docs.text:
wget https://github.com/kartoza/gatus-monitor/releases/latest/download/gatus-monitor_linux_amd64.deb
sudo dpkg -i gatus-monitor_linux_amd64.deb

docs.title: Red Hat/Fedora (.rpm)
docs.text:
wget https://github.com/kartoza/gatus-monitor/releases/latest/download/gatus-monitor_linux_amd64.rpm
sudo rpm -i gatus-monitor_linux_amd64.rpm

docs.title: Binary Installation
docs.text:
wget https://github.com/kartoza/gatus-monitor/releases/latest/download/gatus-monitor_linux_amd64
chmod +x gatus-monitor_linux_amd64
sudo mv gatus-monitor_linux_amd64 /usr/local/bin/gatus-monitor

docs.title: Running
docs.text: gatus-monitor

docs.title: Direct Download
docs.text:
1. Download the appropriate version for your Mac:
  2. Apple Silicon (M1/M2): gatus-monitor_darwin_arm64

  3. Intel: gatus-monitor_darwin_amd64

  4. Make it executable:

chmod +x gatus-monitor_darwin_arm64
  1. Run the application:
./gatus-monitor_darwin_arm64

Note: On first run, you may need to allow the application in System Preferences → Security & Privacy.

docs.title: MSI Installer
docs.text:
1. Download gatus-monitor_windows_amd64.msi
  2. Run the installer
  3. Follow the installation wizard
  4. Launch from Start Menu or Desktop shortcut

docs.title: Binary
docs.text:
1. Download gatus-monitor_windows_amd64.exe
  2. Place it in a folder of your choice
  3. Double-click to run

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Go 1.21 or later
  • Nix with flakes enabled (recommended)

docs.title: Using Nix
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/kartoza/gatus-monitor.git
cd gatus-monitor
nix develop
go build -o gatus-monitor ./cmd/gatus-monitor
./gatus-monitor

docs.title: Without Nix
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/kartoza/gatus-monitor.git
cd gatus-monitor
go mod download
go build -o gatus-monitor ./cmd/gatus-monitor
./gatus-monitor

Note: You'll need to install system dependencies manually (see Building).

docs.title: Verification
docs.text:
After installation, verify the installation by running:

gatus-monitor --version

You should see output similar to:

Gatus Monitor v0.1.0

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: Now that you have Gatus Monitor installed, proceed to the Getting Started Guide to configure your endpoints.
